title: hybanthus filiformis
intro: Hybanthus filiformis, also known as the "Dwarf Blue Lily" or "Water Violet", is a small, aquatic plant that belongs to the Gentianaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hybanthus filiformis include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as acting as a diuretic to help reduce swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the roots to create a tincture.
